creator: greg@veritas.com
text:
I can install FreeBSD from the 3.1 CDROM and the PCNet/PCI Ethernet
adapter works fine, even with custom configured kernels.  But after
running ``cvsup stable-supfile'' and building a new kernel the device
no longer works, giving the message "lnc1: Memory allocated above
16Mb limit" during boot, and "lnc1: Device timeout -- Resetting" during
run time.

How-To-Repeat: Install FreeBSD 3.1 from CDROM: Adapter/driver works fine
Run cvsup stable-supfile.
make installworld
Reboot, build new kernel, install new kernel, reboot
Adapter/driver no longer works
